symbiosis_pathway: Direct solvent recovery and reuse via distillation
taiwan_case_evidence: Linhai Industrial Park — petrochemical to chemical sector solvent recovery loop documented 2019-2023
source: MAESTRI Deliverable 4.3 + Taiwan EPA Industrial Waste Exchange Database
notes: Same-material direct reuse. Highest possible score. Distillation recovery rate >85% reported in Taiwan.

symbiosis_pathway: Scrap metal direct reuse via EAF remelting
taiwan_case_evidence: Linhai Industrial Park — steel/aluminum scrap from fabrication shops fed directly to EAF furnaces
source: World Steel Association 2023 + Taiwan Metal Industries R&D Centre
notes: EAF route standard in Taiwan. Avoids virgin ore extraction entirely.

symbiosis_pathway: Mechanical recycling into secondary resin pellets
taiwan_case_evidence: Taiwan plastics recycling cluster (Tainan/Kaohsiung) — offcut regrind fed back into injection moulding
source: Taiwan Plastics Industry Association 2022
notes: Clean plastic offcut recycled at >80% efficiency in Taiwan industrial clusters.

symbiosis_pathway: Anaerobic digestion producing biogas for heat/electricity
taiwan_case_evidence: Food processing and paper mill sludge digestion plants in Taichung Science Park — biogas recovery operational
source: MAESTRI Case DB + Taiwan EPA Biogas Subsidy Programme 2021
notes: Anaerobic digestion of organic sludge for biogas is actively subsidised under Taiwan's Renewable Energy Development Act.

symbiosis_pathway: Treated process water direct reuse between facilities
taiwan_case_evidence: Hsinchu Science Park — semiconductor facility wastewater treated and sold to neighbouring textile plant
source: Taiwan HIPA (Hsinchu Industrial Park Administration) 2022
notes: Water reuse between adjacent semiconductor and textile facilities is mandatory under Hsinchu Science Park regulations.

symbiosis_pathway: Waste cooling water reuse in adjacent facility HVAC
taiwan_case_evidence: Taiwan Science Parks — chilled water loop sharing between electronics manufacturers
source: Taiwan Science Park Bureau 2023
notes: Cooling tower blowdown reuse documented across multiple Taiwan science park tenants.

symbiosis_pathway: Waste heat from industrial boilers used in adjacent food drying ovens
taiwan_case_evidence: Tainan — steam from petrochemical plant piped to food processing facility for drying operations
source: Taiwan Industrial Symbiosis Network (TISN) pilot 2020
notes: Waste steam utilisation for food drying eliminates natural gas consumption at receiving factory.

symbiosis_pathway: Recovered solvent reused as dyeing auxiliary or cleaning agent
taiwan_case_evidence: Formosa Textile cluster — spent solvents from electronics sector recovered and applied in pre-treatment dyeing bath
source: Taiwan Textile Federation + MOENV Industrial Waste Exchange 2021
notes: Solvent quality must meet textile grade spec; documented partnerships in Tainan/Kaohsiung corridor.

symbiosis_pathway: Waste steam condensate returned as pre-heated process water
taiwan_case_evidence: Standard steam-condensate return loop used across Taiwan heavy industry clusters
source: Taiwan Boiler Safety Association + MAESTRI Toolkit
notes: Condensate return reduces boiler fuel consumption 8-12%. Widely implemented in Kaohsiung industrial districts.

symbiosis_pathway: Direct waste heat transfer via district heating loop or heat exchanger
taiwan_case_evidence: Linhai Industrial Park — inter-facility heat exchanger network connecting steel and chemical plants
source: Linhai IS Network Annual Report 2022
notes: Direct heat transfer via insulated pipework; documented 15

symbiosis_pathway: Treated process water reused in food plant washing and cooling stages
taiwan_case_evidence: Taiwan food processing clusters in Tainan — water recycled between factories after biological treatment
source: Taiwan Food Industry R&D Institute 2021
notes: Requires pH adjustment; water quality must meet food-grade process water standard CNS 10508.

symbiosis_pathway: Composted sludge applied as agricultural soil amendment
taiwan_case_evidence: Food and paper mill sludge composted and sold to farms in Taichung/Changhua agricultural belt
source: Taiwan EPA Waste Compost Certification Scheme + Council of Agriculture
notes: Must pass Taiwan EPA compost quality standard (heavy metals below threshold). Widely practiced in central Taiwan.

symbiosis_pathway: Treated biosolids applied as soil conditioner to farmland
taiwan_case_evidence: Municipal and industrial sludge biosolids land-application programme approved by Taiwan EPA
source: Taiwan EPA Biosolids Reuse Guidelines (2020)
notes: Application requires MOENV permit. Pathogen reduction to Class A/B standard mandatory.

symbiosis_pathway: Scrap metal rebar and structural sections reused in construction
taiwan_case_evidence: Steel offcut from Kaohsiung fabrication shops sold directly to construction companies as secondary rebar
source: Taiwan Construction Research Institute + Steel Recyclers Association 2022
notes: Secondary rebar must meet CNS 560 standard. Well-established market channel in Taiwan.

symbiosis_pathway: Small metal offcuts sold to hardware parts manufacturers for fasteners and brackets
taiwan_case_evidence: Metal stamping offcuts from electronics/auto sector reused by hardware SMEs in central Taiwan
source: Taiwan SME Hardware Cluster (Taichung) 2021
notes: Offcut geometry and alloy must match hardware spec. Active spot-market for metal drops in Taichung.

symbiosis_pathway: Recycled plastic formed into construction composite boards and panels
taiwan_case_evidence: Taiwan plastic recyclers convert mixed PP/PE offcuts into wood-plastic composite decking boards
source: Taiwan Plastic Recycling Association 2022
notes: Requires sorting and cleaning. Output meets CNS 14643 wood-plastic composite standard.

symbiosis_pathway: Blending of compatible sludge streams for co-digestion or joint composting
taiwan_case_evidence: Paper mill and food sludge co-digestion tested at Taichung facility — higher biogas yield from blended feed
source: National Cheng Kung University IS Research Group 2021
notes: Blending requires compatibility check on heavy metal content and C:N ratio. Suitable pairs well documented.

symbiosis_pathway: Solvent used as cleaning or pre-treatment agent in plastic recycling process
taiwan_case_evidence: Spent toluene/MEK from electronics sector used to clean plastic offcut before resin recovery
source: Taiwan EPA Industrial Waste Exchange Pilot 2020
notes: Solvent-assisted plastic cleaning improves recycled resin purity. Requires controlled application and VOC capture.

symbiosis_pathway: Treated grey water reused in textile pre-wash and rinsing baths
taiwan_case_evidence: Tainan textile cluster — biologically treated rinse water from one factory fed to pre-wash stage of neighbour
source: Taiwan Textile Federation Water Reduction Programme 2022
notes: Water quality must meet Taiwan EPA effluent standard for dyeing reuse. pH 6-9 required.

symbiosis_pathway: Sludge dewatering liquid (centrate) treated and recovered as process water
taiwan_case_evidence: Paper mill centrate treated by MBR to reclaim water for non-contact cooling use
source: Taiwan Paper Industry Association + NCKU 2021
notes: Centrate is nitrogen-rich; requires biological nitrogen removal before reuse. Limited to non-potable applications.

symbiosis_pathway: Metal chips used as supplemental fuel in rotary kiln or incineration with energy recovery
taiwan_case_evidence: Metal-contaminated chips with oil residue incinerated with energy recovery in cement kiln
source: Taiwan Cement Industry + MOENV Co-processing Guidelines 2022
notes: Only oil-contaminated metal fines qualify. Clean metal offcut should always go to EAF recycling (score 90).

symbiosis_pathway: Waste plastic used as refuse-derived fuel (RDF) in cement kiln co-processing
taiwan_case_evidence: Non-recyclable mixed plastic converted to RDF pellets used in Hualien cement plant kilns
source: Taiwan Cement RDF Co-processing Programme (MOENV 2021)
notes: RDF co-processing saves ~10% coal in kiln. Restricted to non-PVC plastic under Taiwan regulations.

symbiosis_pathway: Solvent incineration with energy recovery — technically possible but low net value and high risk
taiwan_case_evidence: Rare — only for heavily contaminated unsalvageable solvents; dedicated hazmat incinerator with waste heat boiler
source: MOENV Hazardous Waste Incineration Regulations
notes: Hazardous process. Requires licensed hazmat incinerator. Score reflects high risk and minimal symbiosis value.

symbiosis_pathway: Contamination and reaction risk — solvent residues inhibit biological treatment of sludge
taiwan_case_evidence: No documented Taiwan case — combination flagged as incompatible in MOENV waste mixing guidelines
source: Taiwan MOENV Industrial Waste Mixing Prohibition Table (2019)
notes: Solvent contamination of organic sludge prevents composting and anaerobic digestion. Regulatory restriction applies.

symbiosis_pathway: Corrosion and heavy-metal leaching risk if metal contacts untreated water
taiwan_case_evidence: No Taiwan case documented — metal-water direct contact creates heavy metal contamination in water stream
source: Taiwan EPA Heavy Metal Effluent Standard + IPCC Industrial Ecology
notes: Metal offcut must be kept dry; exposure to water causes oxidation and potential heavy metal (Pb/Cd) leaching.

symbiosis_pathway: Fire and explosion hazard — direct heat application to flammable solvents
taiwan_case_evidence: Prohibited under Taiwan Occupational Safety and Health Act — flash point hazard for common industrial solvents
source: Taiwan OSH Act Article 12 + MOENV Hazardous Waste Regulations
notes: All common industrial solvents (acetone/MEK/toluene) have flash points <60°C. Direct heat exchange prohibited.

symbiosis_pathway: Biological contamination of metal surface — microbes in sludge cause accelerated corrosion (MIC)
taiwan_case_evidence: No Taiwan case — combination flagged by Taiwan Metal Industries R&D Centre as MIC risk
source: Taiwan Metal Industries R&D Centre Corrosion Advisory 2020
notes: Microbially-induced corrosion (MIC) from sulphate-reducing bacteria in sludge damages metal alloy surfaces.

symbiosis_pathway: Plasticiser and additive leaching risk — plastic contact with water streams causes contamination
taiwan_case_evidence: Taiwan EPA flagged plasticiser (DEHP) leaching from PVC offcuts into water — regulated under Toxic Chemical Substances Control Act
source: Taiwan EPA Toxic Chemical Substances Control Act + ECHA REACH
notes: PVC and additive-loaded plastics must not contact water streams intended for reuse. DEHP is listed as SVHC in Taiwan.

symbiosis_pathway: Food safety contamination — any solvent residue in food-contact process water is prohibited
taiwan_case_evidence: Absolute prohibition under Taiwan Food Safety Management Act — no solvent may enter food processing water streams
source: Taiwan Food Safety Management Act (2014 amended 2022) + FDA Standards
notes: Zero tolerance for solvent contamination in food-contact materials. Regulatory enforcement is strict in Taiwan.
